## What is Scoro?

Scoro is a professional services automation (PSA) platform that covers the full project lifecycle from quote to cash. It combines project management, CRM, resource planning, time tracking, budgeting, and invoicing into a single system, eliminating the need for separate tools in each of those categories.

## Who is it for?

Scoro is designed for professional services firms — consultancies, creative agencies, architecture practices, IT services companies, and engineering firms — that bill for their time and need to manage project profitability, utilization, and client relationships in one place. It requires a minimum of five users, making it unsuitable for solo operators.

## Strengths

- **Quote-to-cash in one system** — proposals, project delivery, time tracking, and invoicing are all connected, so nothing falls through the cracks between tools.
- **Real-time profitability** — budget vs. actual tracking at the project and role level gives managers visibility before a project goes over budget.
- **Advanced quoting** — breaks down deliverables by role with full cost and margin visibility at quote time.
- **Resource planning** — capacity forecasting and utilization tracking help managers avoid overloading teams.
- **AI assistant (ELI)** — natural language queries for project data and reporting without manual dashboard building.

## What to consider

- Minimum five-user requirement rules it out for very small teams.
- Implementation takes real effort — the depth of the platform means configuration and data migration require time upfront.
- Primarily built for billable services work; it's not a great fit for product companies or pure sales-led businesses.

## Bottom line

Scoro is one of the most complete PSA platforms available for agencies and professional services firms. If your team runs on billable hours and you're tired of reconciling data across separate project, time, and billing tools, Scoro justifies its price with genuine depth.
